feedback.text: I fully support the objectives of the EU Digital Fairness Act. But I would also like to raise a very concrete concern: In the digital ecosystem, payment service providers such as Mastercard and Visa (non-exhaustive list) have practically become critical infrastructure. These companies have now abused their position to force online platforms to remove lawful content, under threat of stopping their payment services. This censorship imposed by critical infrastructure companies through blackmail harms both consumers and entrepreneurs (e.g. developers of these legal offers). Most other forms of critical infrastructure must not exploit their position of power in such a way. I believe that the same standard should also apply to payment service providers. They should not be allowed to refuse services as long as they are not demonstrably illegal. Extending consumer protection to this area is essential to protect consumers and suppliers and to create and secure a fair and responsible digital marketplace.
